name: United States
summary: Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27; ~48h coverage — no brief ran May 26). **THE US CONDUCTED CONFIRMED SELF-DEFENSE STRIKES INSIDE SOUTHERN IRAN ON MONDAY MAY 25 — THE DOMINANT EVENT — STRIKING TWO IRGC FAST BOATS LAYING MINES IN THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ AND A BANDAR ABBAS SAM SITE (CONFIRMED ACTION — CENTCOM/Hawkins 'self-defense,' 'restraint'; CNN/NBC/Al Jazeera/NPR). THE TARGETING IS THE STORY: THE DEAL REQUIRES IRAN TO *CLEAR* THE MINES IT LAID IN HORMUZ, YET IRAN WAS STILL LAYING THEM AND THE US STRUCK THE BOATS DOING IT. IRAN VOWED RETALIATION BUT HAS NOT ACTED (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT — RHETORIC ONLY). THESE ARE LIMITED SELF-DEFENSE STRIKES INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE, NOT A FULL-SCALE RESUMPTION; TRUMP SIMULTANEOUSLY SAID TALKS WERE 'PROCEEDING NICELY' AND THE DEAL IS 'LARGELY NEGOTIATED' — THE NEGOTIATE-WHILE-BOMBING PATTERN IN ITS PUREST FORM, A SINGLE COERCIVE STRATEGY, NOT TWO CONTRADICTORY DATA POINTS.** (1) **US APPROVED Israel's expanded Lebanon operation [CONFIRMED]** — greenlit the cross-Litani ground op + 100+ strikes on Hezbollah sites, but WARNED Israel against striking in Beirut to protect the Iran talks. (2) **Trump's Camp David Cabinet meeting May 27 [RHETORIC/POSITIONING]**: 'I don't care about the midterms,' confident a deal is near but 'not yet satisfied with the terms — willing to resume fighting if it can't secure its demands.' US reporting now lists three deal-blockers: nuclear, sanctions relief, and Lebanon-coverage. (3) **Abraham Accords demand WIDENED from 6 to 8 nations** — added UAE + Bahrain (both already signatories), framed as 'all simultaneously'; **PAKISTAN REJECTED it** (Defence Min. Asif: joining 'clashes with our fundamental ideologies'); leaders reportedly 'stunned' (Axios single-source). (4) **Deal 'largely negotiated'** (60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clears mines, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers, HEU 'disposal' mechanism, 'strong inspections') but held up by nuclear/sanctions/Lebanon; new Iranian hard lines (Bagheri Kani 'blockade-lifting not yet agreed'; Shamkhani 'fantasy' on nuclear control). **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: (a) the 'converting strike plan to active OR executing strike' trigger MOVES TO ACTIVE — the 'executing strike' limb IS satisfied (CONFIRMED limited self-defense strikes May 25), though these are NOT a full-scale resumption; (b) the Kharg/ground-invasion trigger STAYS WATCHING (no ground action); (c) the six-named-nations Abraham-Accords trigger MOVES TO ACTIVE/FIRED on the rejection limb (Pakistan's public 'no' — CONFIRMED; demand now widened to 8 with UAE + Bahrain). **MAY 25 STRIKES = CONFIRMED ACTION (limited/self-defense); IRAN RETALIATION = RHETORIC ONLY, NOT ACTED.** PRIOR (May 25): **THE US ATTACHED THE LARGEST SCOPE-EXPANSION OF THE CYCLE TO THE IRAN DEAL WHILE HOLDING 'NO RUSH' — AND DOMESTIC SKEPTICISM IS NOW FORMING ON BOTH FLANKS. TRUMP DECLARED IN MONDAY TRUTH SOCIAL POSTS THAT IT 'SHOULD BE MANDATORY' THAT SIX MUSLIM NATIONS (SAUDI, QATAR, PAKISTAN, EGYPT, JORDAN, TURKEY) 'SIMULTANEOUSLY, SIGN ONTO THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS' AS PART OF THE IRAN DEAL, STARTING WITH SAUDI AND QATAR, AND INVITED IRAN INTO THE 'WORLD COALITION' IF IT SIGNS (CONFIRMED — NPR, WaPo, CNBC, ToI, AXIOS; RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND — NO SIGNED ACCORDS). PER AXIOS, THE TARGETED CAPITALS MET IT WITH 'SILENCE ON THE LINE.' SIMULTANEOUSLY SASC CHAIR ROGER WICKER (R-MS) WARNED THAT SEEKING A DEAL NOW RISKS 'A PERCEPTION OF WEAKNESS'; HAWKS AND DEMOCRATS BOTH VOICE 'BAD DEAL' CONCERNS; CNN FRAMED THE POSSIBLE DEAL AS 'ALMOST AS DIVISIVE AS TRUMP'S DECISION TO WAGE WAR' (CONFIRMED — CNN POLITICS MAY 25) — A GENUINE DOMESTIC-POLITICAL CONSTRAINT ON HOW SOFT A DEAL TRUMP CAN SIGN.** (1) **Abraham Accords 'mandatory' demand [NEW, RHETORIC]**: the operative emergence of the Lindsey Graham 'expansionist' vector, now US policy — bolting Arab-Israeli normalisation onto a Hormuz/nuclear de-escalation deal vastly increases its degrees of freedom and hands every reluctant capital a reason to slow-walk; Gulf state media conspicuously did NOT amplify it. (2) **US domestic skepticism [CONFIRMED, domestic-political constraint]**: Wicker's hawkish-skeptic flank (too-soft worry) sits alongside Democrats' restrictionist critique; the Accords condition is partly Trump's answer to the 'perception of weakness' critique. (3) **'No rush' hold + cooling on imminence**: Rubio said talks are 'still a work in progress' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz (CNN liveblog May 25); both sides converging on 'not imminent' (Baghaei: 'no one can claim an imminent agreement is about to be signed'). (4) **Doha venue + money track (CONFIRMED ACTION)**: a senior Iranian delegation (Ghalibaf, Araghchi, central-bank governor Hemmati) arrived in Doha to work Hormuz + HEU + frozen-asset unfreezing with Qatar's PM; Fars says 'Washington has agreed to release part of Tehran's frozen overseas assets' in exchange for lifting Hormuz restrictions (the US is NOT physically at the Doha table — Qatar is shuttling). (5) **Nuclear contradiction persists**: a US senior admin source (Axios) says Iranians gave 'verbal and in writing' assurances any permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium and that Mojtaba 'endorsed the broad template' — contradicted by Baghaei 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage'; the Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is Day 4 of 14 with NO Iranian state-media confirmation/walk-back. **Structural reads**: the kinetic binary is now firmly subordinate to the deal-architecture binary; Monday added a maximalist scope-expansion (Abraham Accords) that the targeted capitals met with silence — most likely an opening-bid overreach, but a potential poison pill that stalls the Iran deal, while Wicker's flank constrains how soft a deal Trump can sign. **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION; MAY 25 IS RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC POSTURE — NEITHER ACCEPT NOR REJECT, NO PIVOT TO KINETIC.** PRIOR (May 24): **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW EXPIRED WITHOUT KINETIC ACTION. WHITE HOUSE OFFICIALS PUBLICLY NAMED MOJTABA KHAMENEI AS DECISION-LOCUS ('SEVERAL DAYS TO APPROVE') — FIRST US-SIDE INSTITUTIONAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF MOJTABA. CBS SENIOR ADMIN SOURCE: IRAN 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED' TO HEU DISPOSAL (CONTRADICTED BY IRAN FM). TRUMP-NETANYAHU PRIVATE CALL: 'WILL NOT SIGN ANY FINAL AGREEMENT WITHOUT DISMANTLEMENT' (CONTRADICTS PUBLIC FRAMING). RUBIO IN NEW DELHI: 'PROGRESS IN PAST 48 HOURS' ON HORMUZ; QUAD MEETING TUESDAY MAY 26. THE US IS DOUBLE-TRACKING: ADMIN-SOURCE 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED' POSITIONS THE DEAL TRACK; TRUMP-NETANYAHU PRIVATE ASSURANCE POSITIONS THE KINETIC FALLBACK; SUNDAY 'NO RUSH' PIVOT RE-OPENED THE TIMELINE.** (1) **Trump Sunday May 24 (CONFIRMED — CBS liveblog, CNBC)**: pivoted from Saturday's 'I'll likely decide tomorrow' to 'time is on our side… not to rush'; US blockade 'will remain in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified and signed.' Largest single-day deal-cycle rhetoric pivot. (2) **White House officials (REPORTED — JPost primary)**: 'did not expect a deal to be reached on Sunday'; Mojtaba 'would take several days to approve any deal.' First US-side institutional acknowledgement of Mojtaba as decision-locus. US 'still obstructing some clauses in the agreement, including the release of Iran's blocked assets.' Negotiations 'still had the possibility of collapsing.' (3) **CBS senior administration source (REPORTED — single anonymous channel)**: US and Iran have reached 'broad commitment on principles' and 'the Iranians have in principle agreed to a deal that would include the disposal of highly enriched uranium' — CONTRADICTED by Iran FM Baghaei 'not yet accepted any actions on its nuclear programme' (JPost). (4) **Trump-Netanyahu private call (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: Trump assured Netanyahu privately he 'will not sign any final agreement without the conditions that Iran dismantle its entire nuclear program and remove all enriched uranium from the country' — directly contradicts admin's public 'in principle agreed' framing. **The double-tracking is the signal**: either Trump is publicly accommodating Iran while privately reassuring Netanyahu, or the admin source over-stated Iranian acceptance to drive deal momentum. (5) **Rubio in New Delhi for Quad bilateral with Jaishankar (CONFIRMED — US News, CNBC)**: 'progress had been made in the past 48 hours on an outline that could help resolve the situation around the Strait of Hormuz.' Bilateral covered Middle East, trade, visas, maritime security, energy. Jaishankar: 'India and US are keen on keeping global maritime trade safe and uninterrupted.' **Quad meeting Tuesday May 26** — India is being briefed on the Hormuz deal contemporaneously with its publication, structural inclusion of India's energy-security stake. (6) **CBS Pentagon channel went silent Sunday on the Memorial Day cancellation framework** — no kinetic-preparation Sunday update, consistent with the May 24 window expiring without kinetic action. (7) **MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW DAY 5 OF 5 RESOLVES UNTRIPPED_EXPIRED**: no kinetic order, no strike directive, no Memorial Day weekend escalation through Sunday evening Tehran time. (8) **Lindsey Graham (R-SC) urged Trump to secure Saudi Abraham Accords as part of negotiations**; Chris Van Hollen (D-MD) criticised deal ('when you're digging a hole, you should stop digging') — bipartisan critique vectors forming (Graham expansionist vs Van Hollen restrictionist) but neither politically operative. (9) **Markets pricing the dual-track at high probability**: Brent $103.54 (-5%+ on the week, cleanest paper-side deal-anticipation print of cycle); VIX 16.70 at year lows; gold $4,523 (-3.9% MoM underperformer); USD/INR ₹95.68. (10) **The bifurcation thesis is now operationally explicit**: the admin's public 'broad commitment on principles' + 'in principle agreed to disposal' framing is the deal-anchoring signal; the Trump-Netanyahu private assurance + Mojtaba 'several days' acknowledgement + 'still obstructing some clauses' is the operational floor. Markets are pricing the public framing; the operational architecture is preparing for a deal-failure outcome. **The convexity is asymmetric to the downside.** PRIOR (May 23): TRUMP MAY 23 ANCHORED DEAL DIRECTION ('EVERY DAY BETTER'; 'GETTING A LOT CLOSER'; URANIUM 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED') WHILE CBS REPORTS US MILITARY/INTEL CANCELED MEMORIAL DAY WEEKEND PLANS — DUAL-TRACK PRE-POSITIONING AS MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW DAY 5 OF 5. RUBIO IN NEW DELHI: 'SOME PROGRESS,' 'A LITTLE BIT OF MOVEMENT.' PAKISTAN'S COAS MUNIR IN TEHRAN ESCALATES MEDIATION TO MILITARY-CHIEF LEVEL.** (1) **Trump May 23 framing** (CBS primary, ToI/Haaretz liveblog primary): US-Iran 'getting a lot closer'; 'every day it gets better and better'; 'we get everything we want'; would only accept deal where 'we get everything'; if no agreement 'no country will ever be hit as hard.' Two non-negotiables: Iran cannot obtain nuclear weapons; enriched uranium must be **'satisfactorily handled'** — meaningful softening from May 16 letter's 'full dismantlement + zero enrichment within 2 months.' Times of Israel framed as '50/50' probability split, 'will likely decide tomorrow.' (2) **Rubio in New Delhi** (Al Jazeera May 22-23 primary, Detroit News May 22 wire): 'some progress' + 'a little bit of movement, and that's good' + 'a chance that, whether later today, tomorrow, in a couple days, we may have something to say.' (3) **CBS Pentagon-source channel May 22-23**: US military and intelligence personnel canceled Memorial Day weekend plans (May 23-25 US) in anticipation of renewed strikes on Iran; Washington 'yet to make a final decision.' Procedural pre-positioning, administrative readiness independent of order — consistent with Trump's May 18 'remain prepared for a full-scale assault at a moment's notice' framing. The same pattern that produced the May 18 postponement: kinetic option administratively pre-staged while diplomatic track runs. (4) **Pakistan's COAS Field Marshal Asim Munir conducted ~24-hour Tehran visit May 22-23** (Al Jazeera primary, ARY News, ISPR primary, Geo TV, Daily Pakistan, Times of Israel) — mediation track structurally escalated from Naqvi Interior Minister to COAS military-chief level. ISPR claimed 'encouraging progress towards a final understanding' (REPORTED framing). Iranian official quoted by Al Jazeera described Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — dual-channel architecture explicit. (5) **Iranian institutional counter-signal at maximum amplitude**: FM Baghaei 'deep and significant disagreements remain' + Ghalibaf 'US not honest partner' + UN mission 'excessive demands' + Tasnim military source 'Iran preparing for resuming fighting' + Baghaei publicly framed '30-60 days' timeline for finalizing MOU details — structurally LONGER than Trump's framing. (6) **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** + urged Iran to 'seize the opportunity to avoid escalation' (Al Arabiya May 22-23 primary) — Gulf editorial coordination intact, Saudi positioning for primary deal credit. (7) **Mojtaba uranium directive Day 2 of 14**: NO Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back; Trump's softer 'satisfactorily handled' formulation is the matching US-side accommodation. (8) **EU moving to sanction Iran over Hormuz blockade** (Al Jazeera May 22 single-source mention, REPORTED LOW-CONFIDENCE pending European-side primary confirmation — Reuters/FT/Politico Europe/Euractiv have not corroborated through May 23 IST). **The May 22 brief's bifurcation thesis is now operationally explicit in Trump's own language**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions track is what he is talking about; nuclear track is what he has quietly softened on. **The cross-side timeline gap** (Trump 'a couple days' vs Baghaei '30-60 days') is the structural mismatch. **Vali Nasr 'time on Iran's side' thesis operationally validated** by Trump's softer uranium framing. **Markets reading the bifurcation cleanly**: Brent flat at $103-104 (May 22 close), VIX 16.70 cycle low (-13.5% MoM), gold $4,521 underperforming (-3.9% MoM, -13.1% 3M) — gold's failure to bid the May 23-25 Memorial Day weekend kinetic window IS the cleanest cross-asset signal that markets do NOT believe Trump pulls the trigger. PRIOR (May 22): **DNI TULSI GABBARD RESIGNED MAY 22 — FOURTH CABINET-LEVEL DEPARTURE OF SECOND TERM, SECOND SENIOR IRAN-SKEPTIC IC DEPARTURE (AFTER JOE KENT'S MARCH NCTC EXIT). PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA MAY 21-22. TRUMP PUBLICLY PIVOTED TO 'IN NO HURRY' + NETANYAHU 'WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' RUBIO MAY 21 HORMUZ RED LINE: DEAL 'UNFEASIBLE' IF IRAN CONTINUES TOLLING SYSTEM. THE KINETIC AXIS IS NO LONGER THE BINDING CONSTRAINT; THE DEAL-ARCHITECTURE AXIS IS, AND IT HAS BIFURCATED.** Across May 20-22: (1) **DNI Gabbard resigned May 22 (CONFIRMED — PBS NewsHour, WHSV/AP wire, NewsNation, MSNBC, ToI liveblog primary)** citing husband Abraham's 'extremely rare form of bone cancer.' Reported context (msnow May 22, REPORTED): 'there had been rumblings that Gabbard would split with Trump after the president's decision to strike Iran. During a congressional hearing in March, her measured comments were notable for their careful non-endorsement of Trump's decision to strike Iran.' Fourth Cabinet-level departure of second term; **Joe Kent, director of National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation in March citing inability to 'in good conscience' back the war on Iran** — second senior Iran-skeptic IC departure inside two months. **The institutional signal**: whatever institutional Iran-skeptic restraint existed in the IC is now substantially weaker — makes a kinetic restart easier IF Trump pivots back to strikes, AND makes a deal acceptance easier IF Trump wants to accept the Pakistan-leaked text without IC pushback. Gabbard's departure REMOVES a brake from both branches simultaneously. (2) **Trump May 20 White House remarks (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS News, CNN, The Week)**: 'in no hurry to reach an agreement,' Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do,' 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire,' was 'an hour away' from ordering new strikes May 18 before Gulf allies asked him to wait. (3) **Pakistan-mediated draft text leaked via Al Arabiya (REPORTED)** — ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions architecture but NO uranium dismantlement. (4) **Rubio May 21 publicly told reporters (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — CBS News primary)**: any deal would be 'unfeasible' if Iran continues pursuing the implementation of a tolling system in the Hormuz waterway. **The cleanest publicly stated US red line on the Hormuz axis of the cycle** — leaked Al Arabiya text addresses Hormuz freedom of navigation but does NOT address Iran's parallel Iran-Oman discussions over a 'permanent toll mechanism.' Two parallel tracks now exist on the Hormuz architecture: (i) leaked deal text's freedom-of-navigation guarantee — US-acceptable, (ii) Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism — Rubio publicly stated US-unacceptable. (5) **Trump-Netanyahu 'difficult' May 20 call (Axios primary scoop)**: Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' after — wanted to continue strikes; Trump explicitly subordinated him on the record. **Markets cascade across May 19-22**: Brent $104.52 May 22 (-5.3% cumulative); WTI broke $100 to $99.50 (-6% two sessions); VIX 16.61 (cycle low -14% MoM); USD/INR retraced from intra-week record low ₹97.15 to ₹95.69 on ~$5B in 48hr RBI intervention. **Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' sustain-rule binary resolves to FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE**: May 21-22 closes decisively below $108 mathematically falsified the May 18-19 2-session sustain rule; StanChart $130-145 path momentum extinguished; Goldman $120 Q3 base case itself re-pricing. **Structural premium intact at $30+ above $73 baseline** even after tail-risk de-pricing $7-8. **The asymmetric path the leak text creates**: ceasefire/Hormuz/sanctions deliverable executable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed by Mojtaba's directive (per nuclear-program node), Israel-frustration framing escalates. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **TUESDAY MAY 19 TRUMP NSC MEETING IS THE OPERATIONAL BINARY** — Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window (closes May 24). Trump-Netanyahu phone call Sunday May 17 + Israeli security cabinet meeting; Trump 'for Iran, the clock is ticking' Truth Social post Sunday (CONFIRMED RHETORIC); Trump expected to convene NSC Tuesday May 19 (REPORTED via Haaretz May 18; no White House public confirmation through Monday morning IST). **Haaretz May 18 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares for Renewed Iran Strikes'** is the cleanest publicly stated US-Israel-divergence framing of cycle. CNN's coverage balanced — Trump's 'decision to make' frame from Friday continues, with Tuesday NSC as operational point. **NPR remains notably absent on strike-preparation specifics through Monday morning** — institutional caution that pairs against NYT's commitment to suggest story is real but at edge of publicly defensible. WSJ/Bloomberg leading with market-pricing frame (Brent above $108 sustained, Sensex sharp lower, INR record low) rather than kinetic-preparation frame. **Markets cascade firing**: Brent $111.47 +2.0% (2-session sustain rule met = StanChart $130-145 path activates); WTI live $108.20 +2.6% (yfinance $103.41 -1.9% is stale-comparator artifact); USD/INR ₹96.29 record low (Day 6 of 5-7 trigger fires); Sensex/Nifty -1.30%/-1.04%; energy XLE +2.4%; defense ITA -3.2% (NOT bidding on prep news = structural de-rating); MSCI EM -3.4%. **Tuesday NSC operational binary**: (a) strike order = Brent +$8-15 to $120-130, VIX 24-28, defense +6-10% snap, S&P -3-5%; (b) public back-off framework = Brent retraces $5-8, VIX 14-16, S&P +1-2%; (c) nothing operational = Branch 2 extended-economic-warfare holds. **PRIOR (May 17): KINETIC PREPARATION POSTURE HARDENS — NYT REPORTS 'MOST INTENSE PREPARATIONS YET' TO RENEW STRIKES ON IRAN 'AS SOON AS NEXT WEEK' (REPORTED, two Middle Eastern officials per NYT national security desk).** NYT scoop (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16) details two options: (i) commando operations to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites — 'thousands of supporting forces to create a perimeter,' US officials acknowledged the operation 'would risk several casualties' and likely engage Iranian ground troops; (ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island (handles up to 90% of Iranian oil exports). Amplified by Times of Israel May 16 liveblog, Jerusalem Post 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' (article 896353), Gulf News 'Operation Epic Fury 2.0,' Fox News 'Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island,' CNN Politics 'Trump returns from China with no Iran breakthrough — and a decision to make' (Pentagon hawks vs Vance-diplomacy track explicitly described). **NO on-record Pentagon/White House confirmation of operation planning.** Iran's countermove already visible (REPORTED — single-source US intel chain via NYT): Iran has moved additional military personnel and air defenses to Kharg Island in recent weeks — operational preparation on both sides. **Trump's posture HARDENED publicly Sunday morning IST**: Truth Social 'It Was The Calm Before the Storm' AI-generated graphic of Trump + US Navy admiral with stormy waters and Iranian-flagged ship (CONFIRMED RHETORIC); second post showed animation of US warship targeting Iranian-flag aircraft with Trump's voice 'Okay, we have it in our sight. Fire!' (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). **The post-summit reversal pattern has now hardened from rhetoric into REPORTED military preparation — the kinetic axis is materially closer to firing than 48 hours ago.** **NPR ABSENCE on the preparation story through Saturday is itself a signal** (NPR's caution + NYT's commitment suggests the story is real but at the edge of what's publicly defensible). **PARALLEL DIPLOMATIC TRACK INTENSIFIES (CONFIRMED ACTION):** Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived Tehran Saturday May 16 on 2-day official visit explicitly described in Pakistani official communications as 'to facilitate' US-Iran peace talks. No US administration response published over the weekend; US side will surface in coming 48 hours. **Markets closed Sunday — operative prices are Friday May 15 closes.** Monday May 18 close above $108 Brent = 2-session-sustain rule met = StanChart $130-145 path activates. **PRIOR (May 16):** TRUMP IMMEDIATELY REVERSED POST-SUMMIT — TRUTH SOCIAL 'MILITARY DECIMATION OF IRAN TO BE CONTINUED' + HANNITY 'I AM NOT GOING TO BE MUCH MORE PATIENT' + DETROIT NEWS 'DID NOT ASK CHINA FOR ANY FAVOURS' (directly contradicting his Wednesday Fox claim Xi offered to broker peace)**; and on **Saturday May 16 Trump confirmed he sent Iran a NEW NUCLEAR PROPOSAL LETTER demanding full dismantling + halt to all enrichment + end of proxy support within 2-month compliance window** (CONFIRMED letter sent — Trump confirmed; CLAIMED letter contents — RedState May 15 + Wikipedia 2025-2026 negotiations summary; primary text not yet surfaced). The Thursday joint statement produced **ZERO operational follow-through** — no HEU-custodian framework, no Chinese security guarantees, no verification architecture. **Three publicly-visible inconsistent US positions in 48 hours**: (a) Wednesday Fox News Xi offered to broker Iran peace + soybean/Boeing commitments; (b) Thursday joint statement strategic-stability framework + Iran no-nuclear-weapon + Hormuz must remain open; (c) Thursday/Friday Truth Social 'decimation to be continued' + Hannity 'not patient' + Detroit News 'didn't ask for favours.' Yesterday's 'Trump fourth reversal' trigger that already fired is now reinforced as a **fifth high-profile reversal** — pattern structurally embedded in cycle (Fortune April 21: 'Trump officials whisper that his Truth Social posts about Iran risk killing peace talks'). **WARSH FED CHAIR TRANSITION EFFECTIVE MAY 16** — Senate confirmed 54-45 on May 13 (CONFIRMED — CNN + NPR + ABA + C-SPAN + WaPo + CNBC); replaces Powell today. Warsh wants to shrink balance sheet and rely on traditional rate tool; 'Investors now expecting Fed to keep rate unchanged for the rest of the year — or even raise rates if inflation worsens' (Yahoo Finance May 13). Trump allies warning Warsh on rate cuts (WaPo May 14). **APRIL PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13) = BIGGEST SINGLE-MONTH JUMP SINCE DECEMBER 2022**; Core PPI +1% (vs 0.4% est); nearly 60% of rise was in services; energy at the root. Combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12, three-year high), the Fed easing path is closed indefinitely. **US MARKETS PRICED THE STAGFLATION REGIME SHIFT CLEANLY ON MAY 15**: S&P -1.24% to 7,408.50 (off Thursday's 7,501.24 record), NASDAQ -1.54% to 26,225.14, Dow -1.07% lost 50K handle (-537.29 pts to 49,526.17), 10Y Treasury +14bp to **4.595% (highest since Feb 2025)**, 30Y +11bp to **5.121% (highest since May 2025)**; Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.' Tech sold off hard (Intel -6%, AMD -5.7%, Micron -6.6%, Nvidia -4.4%). Silver -9.1% to $77.16, Gold -2.6% to $4,555.80, Copper -4.8%, Platinum -4.9%, Uranium URA -4.9%, Aluminum -2.6%. WTI booked +11% weekly to ~$106; Brent +3.3% to $109.26 (first sustained close above $108 since the trigger was added). **The paper-equity leg of the cycle-extreme bifurcation reversed within 24 hours** — paper-equity capitulated, yields surged, tech leadership cracked simultaneously with the Trump-Xi joint statement producing no operational follow-on; the 'one side resolves violently within 5-10 sessions' framing yesterday is now Day 1 of resolution toward crisis confirmation, not peace. **VIX still only 18.43 (+6.8%) — vol complex has NOT capitulated** (sub-20 range; NVDA earnings May 20 keeps vol-sellers active; systematic dealers still long gamma above 7,400 SPX). VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation. **ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 45 DAYS to ~2026-06-29 with Pentagon-led security track to begin May 29** (CONFIRMED — State Dept primary, Bloomberg, PBS, Times of Israel) — State Dept Tommy Pigott called talks 'highly productive'; substantively elevates process from State-led ambassadorial to Pentagon-led security architecture. **B1 SAUDI ARABIA FLOATED MIDDLE EAST NON-AGGRESSION PACT WITH IRAN MODELLED ON 1975 HELSINKI ACCORDS** (FT primary May 14, amplified across MEE, Haaretz, Arab News). EU institutions explicitly backing the proposal. Israeli and US public stance 'unclear.' UAE-Saudi divergence flagged as complication. Trump's 2-month letter window is now **the binding decision-tree input for the next 60 days** (~2026-07-16 ceiling). PRIOR (May 15): **TRUMP-XI SUMMIT DAY 2 PRODUCED JOINT STATEMENT THAT IRAN 'CAN NEVER HAVE A NUCLEAR WEAPON' AND STRAIT OF HORMUZ 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, CBS, CNN Politics, China MFA readout). Joint statement also committed to 'constructive China-U.S. relationship of strategic stability' framework for the next three years. Xi additionally opposed 'militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use. China MFA readout preserves Iran's 'legitimate right to peaceful use of nuclear energy' escape valve in language. **Trump's Fox News follow-on assertions (CLAIMED only, single source, NOT in joint statement text)**: Xi offered to broker peace with Iran, won't supply Iran military equipment, will buy soybeans/oil/LNG + 200 Boeing 737s — analyst skepticism widespread (China's existing soybean appetite limited by cheap Brazilian alternatives per Reuters). **TRUMP 1-WEEK CLOCK NOW DAY 8 POST-CLOSE WITHOUT STRIKE ORDER**; the May 15-20 decision corridor remains open. Pentagon 'Operation Sledgehammer' contingency naming has not been operationally invoked. Vance's softer 'still making progress' framing has not been disputed by White House. **A3 STATE DEPARTMENT 'PRODUCTIVE AND POSITIVE' ON WASHINGTON ISRAEL-LEBANON TALKS DAY 2** (Haaretz May 15 liveblog primary). Karam-Dermer bilateral closing today; April-23-extended ceasefire actually expires Sunday May 17 (NOT May 15 as yesterday's brief recorded — date correction). Reuters/Manila Times pre-positioning expectations for an extended-political-process announcement. **A4 LITANI-INDUCED ISRAELI KIA TRIGGER FIRED DAY 2 OF 14: Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, Golani Brigade 12th Battalion killed by Hezbollah mortar fire May 14** — stress-tests Washington architecture under State Department sponsorship. **Risk-asset complex hit complacency cycle high May 14**: VIX 17.26 (down from 17.87), S&P 500 / NASDAQ Composite / Dow Jones all closed at record highs (S&P above 7,500 first time at record, Dow reclaimed 50,000) on a day with two new kinetic incidents (Hui Chuan + Haj Ali), an Israeli KIA inside the Litani trigger window, Brent +1.11% to $106.89 (refusing to fall on summit), VLCC TD3C confirmed at $467,408/day via Baltic Exchange Week 17. **This is the cleanest paper-physical bifurcation reading of the cycle: paper assets pricing structural-stability + AI-cycle dominance; physical complex (oil, tanker rates, kinetic incidents, SPR diplomacy) pricing intact-blockade + escalation-tail-risk. recent_signals.headline: **US CONDUCTED CONFIRMED SELF-DEFENSE STRIKES INSIDE SOUTHERN IRAN MONDAY MAY 25 — THE DOMINANT EVENT. US forces struck two IRGC fast boats that were LAYING MINES in the Strait of Hormuz and a surface-to-air missile (SAM) site at Bandar Abbas that CENTCOM said was targeting US aircraft (CONFIRMED ACTION — CENTCOM spokesman Timothy Hawkins 'self-defense strikes' to protect troops, Pentagon 'with restraint' given the April 8 ceasefire; CNN, NBC, Al Jazeera, NPR multi-source primary). THE TARGETING IS THE STORY: the deal Iran is negotiating requires it to *clear* the mines it laid in Hormuz — yet Iran was apparently still laying them, and the US struck the boats doing it. Iran's Foreign Ministry called the action 'bad faith and unreliability' and a ceasefire violation; Iran has VOWED retaliation but HAS NOT ACTED (CONFIRMED NON-EVENT — rhetoric only as of May 27). These are LIMITED, self-defense strikes INSIDE the ceasefire, NOT a full-scale resumption: Trump simultaneously said talks were 'proceeding nicely' and the deal is 'largely negotiated' while warning he would go 'back to the battlefront... bigger and stronger than ever before' if there is 'no deal' — the negotiate-while-bombing pattern, a single coercive strategy.** (1) **US APPROVED Israel's expanded Lebanon operation (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel, Euronews, NPR May 26-27)**: Washington greenlit the cross-Litani ground op + 100+ strikes on Hezbollah sites 'in the coming days' but WARNED Israel against striking in Beirut to avoid damaging the Iran talks (a senior Israeli official said targeted assassinations in Beirut are permitted 'if an operational opportunity presents itself'). (2) **Trump's Camp David Cabinet meeting May 27 (CONFIRMED RHETORIC/POSITIONING — NPR, CNN, PBS NewsHour May 27)**: rare Cabinet meeting (attendees reported to include DNI Tulsi Gabbard) during the deal endgame; Trump vowed to strike a favourable deal, said the regime's effort to 'outlast him won't work because I don't care about the midterms,' expressed confidence a deal is near, but cautioned the administration is 'not yet satisfied with the terms — and is willing to resume fighting if it can't secure its demands.' US reporting frames the finalisation as held up by language on the nuclear programme and sanctions relief, plus the Lebanon-coverage dispute. This is the US-side mirror of Iran's 'graveyard' posture — both principals publicly threatening force while negotiators close text: leverage, not breakdown. (3) **Abraham Accords demand WIDENED from 6 to 8 nations; PAKISTAN REJECTED it (CONFIRMED Pakistan rejection — Samaa TV/Asif; Time, Jerusalem Post, KSAT/AP May 26)**: Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Muhammad Asif said joining the Accords 'clashes with our fundamental ideologies' — a flat public rejection. Trump (May 26 Truth Social) said the signatories 'should not be part of this Deal' and that refusal 'shows bad intention,' telling Saudi Arabia and Qatar to 'go first.' The demand widened to 8 (adds UAE + Bahrain, both already signatories), framed as 'all of these countries simultaneously.' Per a US official cited by Axios, the leaders on the weekend call — 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan — appeared stunned' (REPORTED single-source). Qatar/Saudi gave no public response. (4) **Deal 'largely negotiated' but structurally unresolved (CONFIRMED framework, CONTESTED contents — CNBC, Axios, Euronews, CNN, NPR May 24-27)**: Trump said a deal reopening Hormuz is 'largely negotiated.' Draft MOU: 60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clearing mines, US lifting blockade + sanctions waivers, an HEU 'disposal' mechanism, 'strong inspections.' Three explicit sticking points: (i) Hormuz control/status, (ii) the nuclear file, (iii) whether the ceasefire covers Israel's Hezbollah ops (Iran insists Lebanon covered). New Iranian hard lines: Deputy FM Bagheri Kani 'the two sides had not yet agreed on lifting the blockade'; Khamenei adviser Shamkhani called US control over Iran's nuclear programme a 'fantasy'; FM Araghchi unsure a deal is imminent. The Ghalibaf-led delegation returned to Tehran from Doha. **Structural reads**: the strikes (A1) and the Lebanon escalation (A3) both cut against the deal even as it moves *closer* on paper — frame the simultaneity as the signal. **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: the 'converting strike plan to active OR executing strike' trigger MOVES TO ACTIVE (executing-strike limb satisfied — limited/self-defense, NOT full-scale resumption); the Kharg/ground-invasion trigger STAYS WATCHING (no ground action); the Abraham-Accords trigger MOVES TO FIRED on the Pakistan-rejection limb. **MAY 25 STRIKES = CONFIRMED ACTION (limited/self-defense); IRAN RETALIATION = RHETORIC ONLY, NOT ACTED.**

recent_signals.headline: **US ATTACHED A MAXIMALIST 'MANDATORY ABRAHAM ACCORDS' CONDITION TO THE IRAN DEAL — THE LARGEST SCOPE-EXPANSION OF THE CYCLE — WHILE HOLDING 'NO RUSH.' TRUMP NAMED SAUDI, QATAR, PAKISTAN, EGYPT, JORDAN, TURKEY TO 'SIMULTANEOUSLY, SIGN ONTO THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS,' STARTING WITH SAUDI/QATAR, AND INVITED IRAN INTO THE 'WORLD COALITION.' US DOMESTIC SKEPTICISM FORMING ON BOTH FLANKS — WICKER (R, SASC CHAIR): A DEAL NOW RISKS 'A PERCEPTION OF WEAKNESS'; CNN: 'ALMOST AS DIVISIVE AS TRUMP'S DECISION TO WAGE WAR.' RUBIO: TALKS 'STILL A WORK IN PROGRESS.'** (1) **Abraham Accords 'mandatory' demand (CONFIRMED Trump posts + multi-outlet — NPR May 25, Washington Post May 25, CNBC May 25, Times of Israel May 25, Axios May 24; RHETORIC / NEGOTIATING DEMAND, no signed accords)**: Trump declared it 'should be mandatory that all of these Countries, at a minimum, simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords,' naming Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Egypt, Jordan, Turkey, said the expansion 'should start with the immediate signing by Saudi Arabia and Qatar,' and invited Iran to join the 'World Coalition' if it signs. Per Axios's Ravid, Trump floated this on Saturday's leaders' call; the Gulf/Muslim leaders 'were surprised… there was silence on the line.' This is the operative emergence of the Lindsey Graham 'expansionist' vector the May 24 record flagged as not-yet-operative — now US policy — bolting Arab-Israeli normalisation onto the Hormuz/nuclear de-escalation deal and vastly increasing its degrees of freedom. (2) **US domestic skepticism forming on both flanks (CONFIRMED — CNN Politics May 25, CBS/NPR corroboration; RHETORIC / DOMESTIC POLITICS)**: SASC chair Roger Wicker (R-MS) warned seeking a deal now risks 'a perception of weakness'; conservative hawks and Democrats alike voice 'bad deal' concerns; CNN framed the possible deal as 'almost as divisive as Trump's decision to wage war.' This extends the Graham(expansionist)/Van Hollen(restrictionist) framing with a hawkish-skeptic sub-current (Wicker) worried the deal is too soft — a genuine constraint on how soft a deal Trump can sign, and partly the reason Trump bolted on the maximalist Accords condition. (3) **'No rush' hold + cooling on imminence**: Rubio (CNN liveblog May 25) said negotiations are 'still a work in progress,' with 'a pretty solid thing on the table' on Hormuz; Iran FM Baghaei matched with 'no one can claim that… an imminent agreement is about to be signed.' Both sides converging on 'not imminent.' (4) **Doha venue + frozen-asset money track (CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN, May 25)**: a senior Iranian delegation (Speaker Ghalibaf, FM Araghchi, central-bank governor Hemmati) arrived in Doha to work Hormuz + highly-enriched uranium + frozen-asset unfreezing with Qatar's PM; per Fars, 'Washington has agreed to release part of Tehran's frozen overseas assets' in exchange for lifting Hormuz restrictions. **The US is NOT physically at the table in Doha — Qatar is shuttling** — and the central-bank governor's presence confirms frozen-asset mechanics are now a live technical workstream (the admin had named frozen-asset release as the active US-side obstruction). (5) **Nuclear contradiction persists**: a US senior admin source (Axios) says the Iranians gave 'verbal and in writing' assurances any permanent deal will include 'disposal' of all enriched uranium and that Mojtaba Khamenei 'endorsed the broad template' — contradicted by Baghaei 'nuclear isn't part of discussions at this stage.' The Mojtaba HEU-retention directive is Day 4 of 14 with NO Iranian state-media confirmation/walk-back. **Structural reads**: (a) the Abraham Accords demand is most likely an opening-bid overreach, but it raises the activation energy for any signature and is the single largest scope-expansion of the cycle; the absence of enthusiastic Gulf state-media uptake is itself the intelligence that the demand is unwelcome and will be slow-walked; (b) the kinetic binary is firmly subordinate to the deal-architecture binary — Monday's posture was 'no rush' + scope-expansion, neither accept nor reject; (c) Wicker's 'perception of weakness' flank, the Accords poison-pill risk, and the unresolved Hormuz-management/HEU contradictions keep the deal's convexity asymmetric to the downside. **MAY 25 IS RHETORIC / DIPLOMATIC POSTURE — NOT CONFIRMED KINETIC ACTION.**
